name: Coroot Observability API
what_it_does: Open-source eBPF-based observability platform with REST API — providing automatic distributed tracing, service dependency mapping, continuous profiling, and SLO monitoring for Kubernetes without code instrumentation, using eBPF for zero-overhead data collection.
use_cases: Querying service dependency maps and latency data for automated impact analysis
Monitoring SLOs and error rates across Kubernetes services via API
Integrating Coroot observability data into custom dashboards
Querying continuous profiling data for performance regression detection
Building alerting automation on top of Coroot service health data
not_for: Teams needing enterprise SaaS with managed infrastructure (use Datadog or Dynatrace)
Simple log-only monitoring without distributed tracing
best_when: A Kubernetes-native team wants zero-instrumentation observability (eBPF-powered) with automatic service maps, continuous profiling, and SLO monitoring in open-source.
avoid_when: You run non-Kubernetes workloads, need enterprise SaaS support, or don't want to self-host observability infrastructure.

auth.notes: API key or no auth for self-hosted open-source version. SaaS version adds authentication layer. In-cluster deployment — rely on Kubernetes RBAC for access control. No OAuth2 or scopes in open-source version.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: open-source, unlimited clusters, community support
pricing.notes: Open-source Apache 2.0 license. Free self-hosted for all features. Enterprise SaaS for managed deployment and enterprise support. US-based company. eBPF-based collection — no language-specific agents needed. Prometheus metrics backend compatible. Growing CNCF ecosystem adoption.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — rate limits depend on cluster resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for service maps, applications, metrics, and SLOs. Self-hosted deployment means API endpoint varies. No webhooks — polling required. Open-source version has limited auth. Good documentation for core queries. eBPF data collection is the key differentiator.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All API calls are read-only metric queries — fully idempotent. SLO and threshold configuration idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted deployment — no standard SaaS API endpoint
eBPF requires Linux kernel 4.14+ and privileged container access
Default open-source version has no authentication — secure with ingress
API surface limited compared to Datadog or Dynatrace — focused on core observability

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable. Open-source — no default auth (user responsibility). Self-hosted means data never leaves cluster. Apache 2.0 license — transparent codebase. eBPF access requires privileged container — significant security consideration. RBAC via Kubernetes native.
